text: Fill in the blanks about the organs of hearing and equilibrium.
questions: The external auditory meatus is approximately *2.5:length in centimetres* cm long in adults and comprises an outer cartilaginous one-third and an inner *bony:type of the inner two-thirds* two-thirds.
To straighten the EAM for otoscopy in adults, the pinna is pulled *upward:direction* and backward; in children under 3, it is pulled *downward:direction* and backward.
The three ossicles of the middle ear are the *malleus:hammer-shaped bone attached to the tympanic membrane*, incus, and *stapes:stirrup-shaped bone attached to the oval window*.
Myringotomy is performed in the *anteroinferior/posteroinferior:safe quadrant* quadrant of the tympanic membrane to avoid the chorda tympani nerve and ossicles.
The *cochlea:snail-shaped structure in the inner ear* is the organ of hearing, while the *semicircular canals:three loop-shaped structures* detect rotational acceleration for equilibrium.
